2603.18736.title: CausalRM: Causal-Theoretic Reward Modeling for RLHF from Observational User Feedbacks
2603.18736.paper_summary: Despite the success of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) in aligning language models, current reward modeling heavily relies on experimental feedback data collected from human annotators under controlled and costly conditions. In this work, we introduce observational reward modeling -- learning reward models with observational user feedback (e.g., clicks, copies, and upvotes) -- as a scalable and cost-effective alternative. We identify two fundamental challenges in this setting: (1) observational feedback is noisy due to annotation errors, which deviates it from true user preference; (2) observational feedback is biased by user preference, where users preferentially provide feedback on responses they feel strongly about, which creats a distribution shift between training and inference data. To address these challenges, we propose CausalRM, a causal-theoretic reward modeling framework that aims to learn unbiased reward models from observational feedback. To tackle challenge (1), CausalRM introduces a noise-aware surrogate loss term that is provably equivalent to the primal loss under noise-free conditions by explicitly modeling the annotation error generation process. To tackle challenge (2), CausalRM uses propensity scores -- the probability of a user providing feedback for a given response -- to reweight training samples, yielding a loss function that eliminates user preference bias. Extensive experiments across diverse LLM backbones and benchmark datasets validate that CausalRM effectively learns accurate reward signals from noisy and biased observational feedback and delivers substantial performance improvements on downstream RLHF tasks -- including a 49.2% gain on WildGuardMix and a 32.7% improvement on HarmBench. Code is available on our project website.
2603.18736.paper_summary_zh: 儘管從人類反饋中進行強化學習（RLHF）在調整語言模型方面取得了成功，當前的獎勵建模仍然在很大程度上依賴於在受控且高成本條件下從人類標註者收集的實驗反饋數據。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們引入了觀察性獎勵建模——通過觀察性用戶反饋（例如點擊、複製和讚）來學習獎勵模型——作為一種可擴展且具有成本效益的替代方案。<lb/>我們在這一背景下識別了兩個基本挑戰：（1）觀察性反饋因標註錯誤而噪聲較大，這使其偏離了真實的用戶偏好；（2）觀察性反饋受到用戶偏好的影響，用戶傾向於對他們強烈感受的回應提供反饋，這造成了訓練數據和推斷數據之間的分佈偏移。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了CausalRM，一個因果理論獎勵建模框架，旨在從觀察性反饋中學習無偏的獎勵模型。<lb/>為了解決挑戰（1），CausalRM引入了一個噪聲感知的替代損失項，該項在無噪聲條件下明確建模標註錯誤生成過程，並且可以證明等同於原始損失。<lb/>為了解決挑戰（2），CausalRM使用傾向分數——用戶對給定回應提供反饋的概率——來重新加權訓練樣本，從而產生一個消除用戶偏見的損失函數。<lb/>在多個LLM骨幹和基準數據集上的廣泛實驗驗證了CausalRM能有效地從噪聲和偏見的觀察性反饋中學習準確的獎勵信號，並在下游RLHF任務中提供了顯著的性能提升——包括在WildGuardMix上獲得49.2%的增益和在HarmBench上獲得32.7%的改善。<lb/>代碼可在我們的項目網站上獲得。
2603.18736.author: Hao Wang et.al.
2603.18736.authors: Hao Wang, Licheng Pan, Zhichao Chen, Chunyuan Zheng, Zhixuan Chu, Xiaoxi Li, Yuan Lu, Xinggao Liu, Haoxuan Li, Zhouchen Lin
